card.initiative: Supplier-payment focus, event-led acquisition, waiting on pay-to-Chinese-bank product unlock
card.budget: Lean. Central to test ~$300 lead-gen boost for June 9 event.
card.situation: Reality is far more product-constrained than the H2 2025 deck implied. Iris Teo owns TH marketing. Onboarding requires face-to-face verification (Iris: 'it sucks') — same product choke pattern as Philippines. Only 2 sellable products right now: Pay to China + 1688, and payment is Alipay-only — cannot pay Chinese bank accounts yet. Pay-to-Chinese-bank launch is ~a few months out (targeting ~July, per BGO). Iris deliberately holding heavy social/influencer spend until that unlock, to avoid burning budget on a product people try and can't use. Marketing focus is supplier payment only (1688 + B2B direct sourcing recurring), NOT e-commerce collection — Thailand is a LOCAL marketplace market (Lazada/TikTok/Shopee, source from China sell in-country), unlike Vietnam's cross-border e-com base. Importer audience: ~60% pure Thai, ~40% Chinese UBO (Chinese UBO = huge volume, KA-driven not a social channel). Existing WF TH users: ~5% T0-T1 (B2B collection), rest T1-T4 supplier payment. Two events/month requirement (one lead-gen, one WF doc-submission workshop because onboarding is hard); Iris's struggle is filling events.
asks: Set up central-budget lead-gen boost for Iris's June 9 event (~$300 test, Luma-tracked registrations). Check whether central has ad control over WF SEA FB page (Roshini handles it) — likely create a separate post on the global page and boost it for TH region
Wait for pay-to-Chinese-bank product unlock before influencer push. Prep influencer pipeline ~1 month ahead (if July launch, start June)
Explore dedicated Thailand Facebook PAGE (Thai language), Vietnam-style — Iris blocked on content volume (no 1688 video yet, no weekly cadence). Solve via repurposing SEA/global content into Thai
Connect with Iris's local agency (Innosense) if English-speaking, to kick off influencer prep
Iris to send event post + registration link; Iris to confirm pay-to-Chinese-bank timing with BGO
next: Steven sets up June 9 event boost from central budget (new-channel test, no guarantee)
Iris sends event details + Luma registration link
Wait on pay-to-Chinese-bank signal before scaling influencer / paid social
Iris + Rashini producing 1688 how-to videos (account binding is hard) — repurpose for Thai channel
Revisit Thai FB page once content cadence is solvable
benchmarks: April 2026 TikTok KOL test (Innosense agency, 2 micro KOLs): 45,900 combined followers, 2,068 views, 1,729 reach, 197 engagement, 9.53% ER
Top performer longtunhand (24K followers): 1,468 views, 9.26% ER — storytelling/real-user content
Underperformer iiwsnarintorn / Narintorn (21.9K, China-Thai biz CFO): 600 views, 10.17% ER — weak opening, unnatural voiceover
Agency ~$300 buys 20-30 companies to a partnership event (Iris's current benchmark)
TH H2 2025 deck (older context): $80B market, WF 1.1% share, banks ~62%, WISE #4

leadConstraints.onboarding: Face-to-face verification required, small team to do it. Iris: 'to be frank, it sucks.' Same product-choke pattern as Philippines.
leadConstraints.paymentLimitation: Alipay-only. Cannot pay to Chinese bank accounts yet.
leadConstraints.unlockPending: Pay-to-Chinese-bank launch ~a few months out (target ~July, per BGO). The gate for scaling social/influencer.
leadConstraints.marketingScope: Supplier payment only (1688 + B2B direct sourcing recurring). NOT e-com collection.

audienceReality.thailandIsLocalMarket: Local marketplace sellers (Lazada/TikTok/Shopee), source from China, sell in-country. NOT cross-border like Vietnam.
audienceReality.chineseUBO: Huge volume, KA-driven (needs KA team to pitch), not a social/marketing channel. Focus social on Thai-speaking.
audienceReality.existingUsers: ~5% T0-T1 (B2B collection), rest T1-T4 supplier payment (1688 or paying other countries)
audienceReality.collectionMisfit: VA collection doesn't resonate with T3/T4 Thai — they want invoicing / PayPal-like. Big exporters need wire transfer + bank loans/credit (bank competition).

channelReality.youtube_linkedin: Biggest WF followers, but LinkedIn = corporate/employees not SME owners
channelReality.tiktok: Entertainment-skewed, harder to target, but April KOL engagement was strong (9.53% ER)
channelReality.recommendation: TH playbook leans Vietnam-style (own niche, Thai-language dedicated FB page) NOT Singapore/Malaysia (English-OK). Steven recommends a dedicated Thai FB PAGE.

kols.name: ลงทุนแฮนด์ Longtunhand
kols.verdict: TOP — storytelling, real user experience, feels meaningful not promotional

kols.name: Narintorn (王娜琳) — China-Thai biz, CFO/COO PrimoLive
kols.verdict: UNDERPERFORMER — unengaging opening, unnatural voiceover, uninteresting editing

aprilKolCampaign.sentiment: Generic + positive. Comments express interest in the app + appreciation for KOLs.
aprilKolCampaign.agencySuggestion: Multi-platform expansion, more audio-visual TikTok volume with strong hooks
aprilKolCampaign.trendTarget: KOLs who teach selling internationally (Amazon/TikTok Shop), import-export entrepreneurs with real multi-currency experience, freelancers receiving international payments, SME founders

eventMotion.structure: Event 1 = lead-gen (partnership team / hired organizer). Event 2 = WF workshop for doc submission (because onboarding is hard).
eventMotion.nextEvent: June 9 2026 — central boost test (~$300, Luma-tracked) vs the usual $300-to-agency-for-20-30-companies

sources.title: TH KOL Report (Innosense, April 2026 TikTok campaign)
